arrays.definition: A collection of elements stored at contiguous memory locations

python.array_operations:
arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
arr.append(6)  # Add element
arr[2] = 10    # Update element
arr.pop()      # Remove last element
len(arr)       # Get length

linked_lists.definition: A linear data structure where elements are stored in nodes, each pointing to the next node

types.singly_linked_list: Each node has data and pointer to next node
types.doubly_linked_list: Each node has pointers to both next and previous nodes
types.circular_linked_list: Last node points back to first node

operations.insertion_at_end: O(n) for singly, O(1) with tail pointer

python.reverse_list:
def reverse_list(head):
    prev = None
    current = head
    while current:
        next_temp = current.next
        current.next = prev
        prev = current
        current = next_temp
    return prev

stacks.definition: Linear data structure following LIFO (Last In First Out) principle

operations.isEmpty: O(1) - Check if stack is empty

queues.definition: Linear data structure following FIFO (First In First Out) principle

types.circular_queue: Last position connects to first position
types.priority_queue: Elements with higher priority are dequeued first
types.deque: Double-ended queue - insert/remove from both ends

operations.isEmpty: O(1) - Check if queue is empty

python.queue_implementation:
from collections import deque

queue = deque()
queue.append(1)  # Enqueue
queue.append(2)
queue.popleft()  # Dequeue
queue[0]  # Front element

trees.definition: Hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes connected by edges
terminology: Height: Longest path from root to leaf
Depth: Length of path from root to node

types.binary_tree: Each node has at most 2 children
types.heap: Complete binary tree with heap property

graphs.definition: Non-linear data structure consisting of vertices (nodes) and edges
terminology: Undirected graph: Edges have no direction
Cycle: Path that starts and ends at same vertex

representations.adjacency_list: Array of lists for each vertex

shortest_path.dijkstra: Finds shortest path from source (non-negative weights)
shortest_path.bellman_ford: Handles negative weights, detects negative cycles

hash_tables.definition: Data structure that stores key-value pairs using hash function
components: Hash function: Maps keys to indices
collision_resolution: Chaining: Linked list in each bucket
Open addressing: Find next empty slot
Double hashing: Use second hash function

heaps.definition: Complete binary tree with heap property

python.heap_operations:
import heapq

# Min heap (default)
heap = []
heapq.heappush(heap, 3)
heapq.heappush(heap, 1)
heapq.heappush(heap, 2)
smallest = heapq.heappop(heap)

# Max heap (using negatives)
max_heap = []
heapq.heappush(max_heap, -3)
heapq.heappush(max_heap, -1)
largest = -heapq.heappop(max_heap)

bubble_sort.description: Repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if in wrong order

selection_sort.description: Selects minimum and places at beginning

insertion_sort.description: Builds sorted array one element at a time

merge_sort.description: Divide and conquer, splits and merges

quick_sort.time_complexity: O(n log n) average, O(n²) worst

binary_search.description: Requires sorted array, divides search space in half

interpolation_search.description: Estimates position based on value distribution

dynamic_programming.definition: Method for solving complex problems by breaking into simpler subproblems

greedy_algorithms.definition: Makes locally optimal choice at each step hoping for global optimum

backtracking.definition: Algorithmic technique for solving problems recursively by trying to build a solution incrementally

time_complexity.definition: Measure of time taken by algorithm as function of input size

space_complexity.definition: Measure of memory used by algorithm
components: Input space - space for input data

two_pointers.description: Using two pointers to traverse array/list

sliding_window.description: Maintaining a window that slides over the array

fast_slow_pointers.description: Two pointers moving at different speeds

cyclic_sort.description: Placing numbers at their correct indices

tree_bfs.description: Level by level traversal of tree

modified_binary_search.description: Binary search on non-traditional search spaces

examples: Smallest Range Covering Elements from K Lists

arrays: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
linked_lists: Detect Cycle in a Linked List
Remove Nth Node From End of List

tips: Start with brute force, then optimize
Think about time and space complexity
